rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope before use.
When AGREE reports type errors, trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and resolve the root cause.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference features or variables that are explicitly declared or visible in the current component scope.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output features or auxiliary variables only.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have valid types; avoid using identifiers that resolve to <error>.
Use assign for implementation-level signal routing only when the left-hand side is an output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE diagnostics; instead, fix the root reference or type issue.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; they must be in component type contracts.
